title: Earl Grey Chia Pudding with Wild Blueberry Compote

Wild Blueberry-Cardamom Compote: 10 oz. frozen wild blueberries, about 2 1/3 cups and no need to thaw
1 tsp. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract
Earl Grey Chia Pudding: 2 cups milk of choice, such as unsweetened almond milk
1 cup plain 2% or full-fat Greek yogurt
1 tsp. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract

steps: Prepare the wild blueberry compote by combining the frozen wild blueberries, maple syrup, and water in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce the heat, and simmer until some of the berries burst and the mixture thickens, about 10 minutes.
Remove the compote from the heat and stir in the vanilla and cardamom. Transfer to a bowl and let it cool completely at room temperature or in the refrigerator.

Warm the milk in a small saucepan or microwave-safe bowl just until bubbles form around the edge and steam begins to rise. Add the Earl Grey tea bags and steep for 5 to 7 minutes, then remove the bags and let the milk slightly cool.

In a large bowl, combine the chia seeds, Greek yogurt, maple syrup, vanilla, and salt. Pour in the Earl Grey-infused milk and whisk well to combine. Let it rest for 10 minutes, then whisk again to break up any clumps.

Assemble the jars by layering the blueberry compote and chia pudding mixture evenly into 3 glass jars or small containers with fitted lids. Start with about 1 to 2 tablespoons of compote at the bottom and alternate layers from there.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 3 hours, or ideally overnight, until thickened. Enjoy within 4 days.
